title: Global Markets Weekly Update
summary: U.S. equity indices closed higher in a holiday-shortened week: S&P 500 7,500.58 (+0.93%), Nasdaq 26,517.93 (+2.43%), DJIA 51,564.70 (+0.71%). US-Iran MOU sent oil lower and boosted risk sentiment. Fed held at 3.50-3.75% but hawkish dot plot: 9 of 18 officials pencil in at least one 2026 hike. BOJ raised to 1%, BOE/SNB held, Norges Bank signaled hike. China retail sales contracted for first time since late 2022.

title: Treasury Yields Snapshot: June 18, 2026
summary: 10-year Treasury yield finished June 18 at 4.46%, 2-year at 4.19%. 30-year fixed mortgage rate 6.47% (Freddie Mac). Yield curve analysis: 10Y-2Y spread continuously negative Jul 2022-Aug 2024; last negative Sep 5, 2024. Historical recession lead times from first negative spread average 48 weeks.

title: U.S. Economic Calendar — MarketWatch
summary: Full economic calendar for weeks of Jun 15-19 (actual), Jun 22-26 (forecast), and Jun 8-12 (prior). Key next week: Tue S&P flash PMIs, Thu PCE index + GDP revision + durable goods + Williams/Goolsbee speeches, Fri consumer sentiment final.

title: These Wall Street Experts Think the Market Outlook for 2026 Just Got Brighter
summary: Wells Fargo raised S&P 500 year-end target from 7,300 to 7,950 citing Iran peace deal. Evercore ISI bull case 9,000 on SpaceX IPO catalyst. UBS target 7,900 but warns new White House directive on Anthropic models could weaken semiconductor demand confidence. Wedbush calls IPO a 'Goldilocks outcome' for tech. Fed rate decision expected same day.

title: AAII Sentiment Survey: Pessimism Draws Back
summary: Bullish sentiment increased 6.2 pp to 36.6%. Neutral increased 2.1 pp to 24.1%. Bearish decreased 8.3 pp to 39.4%. Bull-bear spread increased 14.5 pp to -2.8%, below historical average of 6.5% for the 18th time in 19 weeks. Special question: small-cap stocks expected to outperform by 27.1% of respondents.

title: Instant View: Fed holds steady in Warsh's debut, but hawkish shift fuels bond-market rout
summary: Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% unanimously. Nine officials project at least one 2026 hike. Warsh dismantled forward guidance: 'radically simplified' statement, downplayed dot plot ('pencils with big erasers'), announced 5 task forces. S&P 500 fell 1.3%, Nasdaq 1.5%. 2Y yield +17 bps to 4.216%. Rate futures priced 72% chance of hike by October. DXY +0.9% to 100.47.

title: Markets are set for a much more hawkish Warsh Fed than expected
summary: Warsh used 'price stability' 12 times in 40-min press conference. July meeting hike probability ~33%. September hike 67% (CME FedWatch). Market-implied fed funds May 2031 at 4.78% (implies up to 5 hikes). Yardeni: 'We thought he was a dove... Instead, he hammered home a strict, orthodox message.' Gasoline dipped below $4/gallon. S&P GSCI down 17% from May peak.

title: SpaceX stock gains 20% in first full day of trading
summary: SpaceX (SPCX) IPO priced at $135/share, closed day one at $161 ($2T+ market cap), surged 20% Monday to $192.50. Biggest IPO in history, raised $75B. Musk: '$1 trillion revenue in 2030.' CFRA sell rating ($115 target). Morningstar values at $63/share. NewStreet Research: $165 target, '10-year lead in launch capabilities.' 2025 revenue $18.7B, net loss $5B.

title: Weekly Market Performance | June 18, 2026
summary: S&P 500 +0.81% for week, Nasdaq +2.14%, DJIA +0.85%, Russell 2000 +0.73%. IT +2.89% best sector; Energy -6.57% worst. Oil -9.71%, gold +0.05%, silver -3.08%. Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate -0.09%. 2Y/10Y curve flattened to ~25 bps. MSCI EM +4.24% week, +29.34% YTD. TIPS breakevens imply Fed achieves 2% inflation within 2 years.

title: Oil settles at three-month low after Trump says deal signed to end Iran war
summary: Brent settled at $83.17/bbl (-4.76%), WTI at $80.75 (-4.87%). US-Iran MOU signed. Hormuz reopening within 30 days. 14M bpd shut during ~3-month closure (~14% of world demand). Citi: Q3 Brent $75, Q4 $70. SPR at 340.3M bbl (lowest since 1983). Israel military to remain indefinitely in Lebanon/Syria/Gaza security zones.

title: Making flexibility count in today's corporate credit markets
summary: IG OAS ~110 bps near all-time tights. Multiverse Corporate yield-to-worst close to 5%. Favoring BBBs/BBs with short duration. Extended allocations toward high end of range; EM corporate at highest ever. Active credit hedges maintained. US utility hybrids attractive: defensive fundamentals + strong income in major investment cycle (grid, AI data centers, electrification).

title: Magnificent Seven Stocks Are Market Drags
summary: All 7 Mag 7 stocks negative in June: NVDA -2.8%, GOOGL -5.4%, TSLA -6.7%, AAPL -6.7%, META -10.4%, AMZN -11.9%, MSFT -13.2%. MAGS ETF -8.2%. Equal-weight Nasdaq-100 (QQQE) +0.3% in June, +17% YTD. Russell 2000 and RSP hit record highs. AVGO -14.5% in June after -22% post-earnings drop.

title: Magnificent 7 Weekly Report - Tesla Needs A New Catalyst
summary: Mag 7 weekly: AAPL +1.7% ($295.95) on AI upgrade bets, GOOGL +1.6% ($363.79) on cloud growth, NVDA flat ($207.41), AMZN -0.4% ($237.50), TSLA -2.5% ($396.38), MSFT -3.1% ($378.91), META -6.2% ($567.58). Meta fell 5.44% in single session Jun 17, erasing $180B market cap. Alphabet announced $84.75B equity raise for AI data centers. Total Mag 7 market cap erased >$2T in first two weeks of June.
